generated_by: Generated by ops/scripts/compile-content.mjs. Do not edit by hand.

interviews.title: Why World War 3 Is Becoming a Structural Fight
interviews.source_title: Professor Jiang: World War 3 Is About To Begin, Let Me Explain!
interviews.dek: Jiang treats the Middle East conflict and global monetary system as parts of one strategic architecture: empire, geography, and control of energy channels.

interviews.title: "Heading For A Global DEPRESSION!"
interviews.source_title: \"Heading For A Global DEPRESSION!\" Professor Jiang Debates Iran War, Trump And China Ties
interviews.dek: Jiang reframes Hormuz disruption as a production-system collapse and argues that escalation incentives make the Iran conflict a political-economic choke point beyond price shocks.

interviews.title: The War Continues, So Financial Power Keeps Its Levers
interviews.source_title: 🔴 Prof Jiang & Alex Krainer - The Elites Exit Plan REVEALED
interviews.dek: Jiang frames the Iran conflict as a managed long war: visible ceasefires do not remove structural incentives that keep military pressure, debt extraction, and elite coordination in place.

interviews.title: Iran as Recalibration, Not Exit
interviews.source_title: Prof. Jiang: Iran Has Trump TRAPPED! Full Interview | Redacted w Clayton Morris
interviews.dek: Jiang treats the Iran shock as a long-cycle pressure system: initial strikes fail, the state shifts to durable economic coercion, and public attention is expected to absorb scarcity, distraction, and control mechanisms as this drags on.

interviews.title: The Final Days of the U.S. Empire
interviews.source_title: The Final Days Of The U.S. Empire! – Full Interview w/ Professor Jiang
interviews.dek: The interview frames U.S. action in Iran as a strategic shift toward prolonged strategic pressure rather than a clean ceasefire, then extends the pattern to bureaucratic overreach, surveillance infrastructure, and social fragmentation while proposing organized labor as the practical counterweight.

interviews.title: Why This Iran War Feels Like a Bear Trap
interviews.source_title: Is the Iran War ACTUALLY Over? w/ Professor Jiang: LIVE 6 pm PST
interviews.dek: Jiang frames the Iran war as a structural problem: empires that enter forceful conflicts without strategic reserve burn out, and the current administration is trying to steer around collapse, domestic optics, and a volatile coalition.

interviews.title: The Strait Is the Nuclear Weapon
interviews.source_title: “It's An Act Of WAR!” Professor Jiang vs Gordon Chang On China, Iran & Trump | Plus Robert Pape
interviews.dek: The interview begins as a fight over whether the Iran war has helped anyone, then turns into a harder question: what happens when a regional war reveals that waterways, energy corridors, diaspora hopes, and presidential threats are all weaker than the physical chokepoint underneath them?

interviews.title: The World Runs on Borrowed Time
interviews.source_title: 🔴 Jiang Xueqin Warns: The End of The World Has Begun (Here's Why)
interviews.dek: The interview starts with a ceasefire question and ends in a resource apocalypse. Jiang's answer is that the Iran war is no longer only about Iran: Hormuz, Malacca, Venezuela, the Levant, NATO, China, and the American home front are all pieces of a world where empire is moving from consent to force.

interviews.title: Empire Is Evil, but It Pays
interviews.source_title: SNEAKO X Professor Jiang X Dave Smith | Unity Amidst Chaos - Full Panel Discussion
interviews.dek: Dave asks Jiang what he is missing. Jiang's answer is that the Iran war cannot be explained only by Trump, Netanyahu, or the Israel lobby. Those actors matter, but the deeper machine is imperial decline: an empire whose beneficiaries know it is evil, know it pays, and cannot make the sacrifices needed to leave it.

interviews.title: Every Technology Needs a Front Man
interviews.source_title: \"They Created Bitcoin!\" Professor Jiang Exposes Why Every Technology Needs A \"Front Man\"│Jack Neel
interviews.dek: The interview starts with the end of the world and Satoshi Nakamoto, but the deeper line is Jiang's theory of front men. Technologies, billionaires, religions, secret societies, and even public intellectuals need a face that makes hidden power feel personal, cute, rebellious, or divine.

interviews.title: The End Times Are A Geopolitical Machine
interviews.source_title: SNEAKO X Professor Jiang X Aleksandr Dugin | Full Interview
interviews.dek: This is not a neutral theology panel. Sneako brings Jiang and Dugin together because eschatology has become a way to read geopolitics: Russia, Persia, Zionism, American Protestantism, China, finance, Epstein, Trump, and the Middle East all become signs in a fight over the end of history. Jiang supplies the machinery: the Bank of England turns capital into empire, Calvinism turns wealth into chosenness and fear, Christian Zionism makes Zionism into an instrument, and Greater North America becomes America's possible last fortress against Eurasia.

interviews.title: When the Policeman Becomes the Pirate
interviews.source_title: Jiang Xueqin: The Iran War & the Battle for the Petrodollar
interviews.dek: The interview begins with Iran and the petrodollar, but Jiang's answer keeps widening. Hormuz becomes Malacca. Sanctions become forced treasury purchases. Tariffs become naval tolls. Trump becomes less a sovereign actor than the agent a declining empire uses when consent stops working.

interviews.title: Grand Strategy Or Pawnhood
interviews.source_title: Exclusive Interview with Professor Jiang by Dr James Cheong 江学勤独家专访 详细分析美国 伊朗 日本 全世界
interviews.dek: A university lecture becomes a warning to China: tactics, utility, and clever people are not enough. A country without a theory of the world is not a player in history. It is the pawn of those who have one.

interviews.title: The Empire Has No Easy Off-Ramp
interviews.source_title: Jiang Xueqin Finally Breaks His Silence With PBD | PBD #772
interviews.dek: PBD brings Jiang on to challenge the viral Iran prediction. Jiang answers with an empire-decline model: late empires start wars they think will be cheap, then discover that the terrain, the currency system, the oil order, and the enemy’s appetite for sacrifice are stronger than the television script.

interviews.title: When War Becomes a Script and Fame Becomes a Trap
interviews.source_title: SNEAKO interviews Professor Jiang: Global Crisis Incoming
interviews.dek: Sneako presses Jiang after the Iran war turns him into a sudden internet figure. Jiang answers with a warning: war does not merely kill abroad. It scripts civil discord, rewards religious extremism, turns media personalities into controlled assets, and tempts the teacher to become a player in the very game he is trying to explain.

interviews.title: Creativity Is A Scientific Rebellion
interviews.source_title: Professor Jiang on teaching for creativity, agency, and empowerment @PredictiveHistory
interviews.dek: Jiang's education argument begins with a narrow definition and ends with a democratic dream. Creativity is not vibes, artiness, or brainstorming. It is the scientific process inside the classroom: evidence, experiment, mistake, feedback, and the courage to stop obeying authority. That is why Chinese schooling is so hard to change. The obstacle is not only bad pedagogy. It is a whole ecology of teacher prestige, parental fear, test addiction, and top-student narrowness.

interviews.title: Useful Idiot, Predictive History, And The Facts Trap
interviews.dek: Mehdi Hasan does not let Jiang enjoy the Nostradamus frame. He tests the method, the misses, the China question, the Professor persona, the facts-versus-truth distinction, and the language around Jews, Israel, and empire.

interviews.title: The Test Is Not The Truth
interviews.source_title: Education in China (Sinica interview with Jiang Xueqin)
interviews.dek: Shanghai can win PISA and still not prove that its schools are forming whole people. Jiang turns the envy around: every test tests test-taking, Gaokao survives because China lacks trust, and the student trained to obey may break when the classroom asks him to speak.
